article_title: Skyroot Aerospace Closes $60 Million Round and Becomes India's First Space-Tech Unicorn Weeks Before Vikram-1's Maiden Orbital Launch
summary: Duplicate of already-published Skyroot funding article (PR #1196, merged earlier in this batch). The work is discarded under v3.9.0; not eligible for rewrite.

findings.details: Already-published article: src/content/articles/2026-05/08-skyroot-aerospace-becomes-indias-first-space-tech-unicorn-with-60-million-round-ahead-of-vikram-1-maiden-orbital-launch.md (originally PR #1196). Same Skyroot $60M round, same $1.1B valuation, same Vikram-1 maiden-orbital framing, same May 7 2026 announcement.

content_preview.title: Skyroot Aerospace Closes $60 Million Round and Becomes India's First Space-Tech Unicorn Weeks Before Vikram-1's Maiden Orbital Launch
content_preview.summary: Hyderabad-based Skyroot raised $60 million co-led by Sherpalo Ventures and GIC at a $1.1 billion pre-money valuation, capping the round just weeks before its three-stage Vikram-1 rocket attempts India's first private orbital launch from Sriharikota.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Skyroot Aerospace has raised about $60 million at a $1.1 billion pre-money valuation, becoming India's first space-tech unicorn ahead of the maiden orbital flight of its Vikram-1 rocket, [TechCrunch reported](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/07/indias-first-space-tech-unicorn-emerges-as-skyroot-gears-up-for-orbital-launch/) on May 7, 2026. The Hyderabad startup, founded in 2018 by former Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) engineers Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka, ex...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively well-written. Different source set from PR #1196 (uses TechCrunch + Tribune + The Next Web vs. PR #1196's TechCrunch + Tribune), and explicitly flags a payload-spec disagreement (350 kg vs 480 kg). On its own merits would APPROVE.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Same Skyroot funding facts as the already-published article.
recommendations: Bot operations: parallel write-article agents need topic-collision detection that includes in-flight submissions.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT under v3.9.0. PR #1196 covers this disclosure.
